Sandbox_BetterSneak_CamoBonus_tooltip: Wearing clothing with similar colors to your enviroment will give you a bonus equal to this amount. How it works: Wearing black, grey or any dark piece of clothing will give you a camo bonus if you are standing in an Urban area. Same applies to wearing green (not too bright) while standing on any kind of vegetation. Wearing dark clothing AND the respective color of your environment will give you both bonuses. You can check what type of area you are in the Search Mode window.
Sandbox_BetterSneak_ClothingAffectsSneak: If enabled, wearing dark clothing will give you a small bonus in sneak and wearing a High vis vest will give you a huge penalty.

Sandbox_BetterSneak_FogBonus_tooltip: When there is a fog, it will reduce the zombies spotting range by this amount.
Sandbox_BetterSneak_Frequency_tooltip: The lower the number, the faster the mod will calculate lightning and stuff(this will affect performance), the greater the number, the longer the mod will wait before doing another calculation.
Sandbox_BetterSneak_LightLevel_tooltip: Lightning must be below this number for this mod to trigger. In other words, if the light surrounding you is above this value, it means there is too much light. If it goes below this value it means it is dark enough for the mod to trigger. 0 is total darkness, pitch black, while 1 is broad daylight.

Sandbox_BetterSneak_MinimumDistance_tool: Sometimes, there will be times where you can have too many bonuses (like a character with level 10 in sneaking standing near a wall during a fog while wearing dark clothes and having the Inconspicuous trait), this value is to keep the zombies being able to see no matter what if something is close enough equal to this distance.

Sandbox_ETW_AffinitySystemGainMultiplier: How much easier it is to gain perks that have Affinity System functionality (only affects perks you started with!). Setting it to 1 would mean that it doesn't make it easier to gain. Setting it to 2 means it'll take twice as fast to gain them. Setting it to 1.5 would make it 50% faster, and so on. So if you start with a positive dynamic trait and later lose it, you'll get it back much faster. But it also affects negative traits!
Sandbox_ETW_AffinitySystemLoseDivider_to: How much harder it is to lose perks that have Affinity System functionality (only affects perks you started with!). Setting it to 1 would mean that it doesn't make it harder to lose. Setting it to 2 means you'll be twice as slow to lose them. Setting it to 1.5 would make it 50% slower, and so on. So if you start with a positive trait that you can lose, it'll be much harder to lose it. But it also affects negative traits!
Sandbox_ETW_AffinitySystem_tooltip:
Defines if the Affinity System is enabled for some traits tied to systems and some complex ones (for example, weight Fear of Locations system, Herbalist, or Sleep System. Check which Systems and Traits are subject to the Affinity system in Google Sheets).
The Affinity system makes it easier to hold onto traits you picked during character creation, both negative and positive. With it enabled, you halve (modifiable) the speed at which you lose these traits, and double (also modifiable) the speed at which you earn these traits
Sandbox_ETW_AsthmaticCounter_tooltip: The value on the counter you should reach to lose the asthmatic trait. Running or exercising in cold temperatures decreases the counter; the colder it is, the faster the decrease. If you're outside, it decreases even faster. If you are a smoker, it decreases even faster. Additionally, being a smoker slowly decreases the counter when you run, even if the weather is fine. Reaching the counter removes the trait. The counter slowly increases by itself as long as you're not running or sprinting and breathing air warmer than 0 degrees Celsius. On top of that, it increases based on your Fitness level and current endurance level. Being a smoker halves the increase rate. Having the asthmatic trait halves it again

Sandbox_ETW_BloodlustMultiplier_tooltip: Multiplies the effect of bloodlust by this value. By default, unhappiness, stress, and panic are each reduced by 4% for each kill. This multiplier is applied to those 4%. So setting this to 0.5 would make those stats reduced by 2% instead
Sandbox_ETW_BloodlustProgress_tooltip: How much total bloodlust progress you need to get the trait. Be aware that you also lose bloodlust if this meter ever falls below 50%. So if you set it low, it's going to be easy to earn but also easy to lose the perk. Progress is capped at 2x the Bloodlust Progress value
Sandbox_ETW_Bloodlust_tooltip: This allows you to gain a benefit by filling and maintaining a bloodlust meter. The bloodlust meter is soft-capped at 72 hours and can be filled by killing zombies. The closer the zombies are, the more quickly the meter will fill. When you're above half of the meter, every hour it will give you progress towards the bloodlust perk; when it's below, it'll remove progress. The bloodlust meter naturally decays by 1 every hour. Having bloodied clothes helps the process
Sandbox_ETW_BodyworkEnthusiastRepairs_to: The percentage of car condition you have to repair to qualify for the perk. Works on every part of a car and is saved between all cars
Sandbox_ETW_BraverySystemKills_tooltip:
Here you set up how many kills you need to get to max out your bravery system. Melee kills count twice for progress. Progression is as follows: Cowardly -> Fear of Blood -> Pacifist -> Adrenaline Junkie -> Brave -> Desensitized
You need to have previous trait gone (if it's negative) or present (if it's positive) to earn/lose next trait In progression.
Amounts to reach each stage are as follows:
Cowardly: 10% of max kills
Fear of Blood: 20% of max kills
Pacifist: 30% of max kills
Adrenaline Junkie: 40% of max kills
Brave: 60% of max kills
Desensitized: 100% of max kills
Example: Max kills is set to 1000. You start with Cowardly. You need 100 kills to remove it. If you get 50 melee kills, it'll be removed. The same is true if you get 40 melee kills (x2 = 80 kills for the counter) and 20 firearms kills
Sandbox_ETW_BraverySystemRemovesOtherFea: Bravery System Removes Other Fear Perks
If enabled, getting desensitized through the Bravery system will also remove other fear perks like Pluviophile, Homichlophobia, Agoraphobic, and Claustrophobic, and prevent you from gaining them
Sandbox_ETW_BurglarSkill_tooltip: Mechanics + Electrical + Nimble. Please note that you need to have at least 2 levels in both Mechanics and Electrical
Sandbox_ETW_CatEyesCounter_tooltip: This allows you to gain points by seeing and observing squares within a certain radius of your character. The number of points you earn is influenced by factors such as the time of day, the darkness of the square, and whether you and the square are inside or outside. The more squares you see and the darker they are, the more points you will earn. Having Eagle Eyed doubles the gain. Making a kill at night triggers extra execution of function responsible for gaining progress (so instead of getting progress 1 time / minute you can get progress 3 times per minute, for example)
Sandbox_ETW_DelayedTraitsSystemDefaultDe: Default delay (in hours) for traits to appear/disappear. For example, if set to 240, when you qualify for a new trait, you'll have a 1 in 240 chance to gain/lose it in the first hour. If it fails, the chance becomes 1 in 239 the next hour, and so on
Sandbox_ETW_DelayedTraitsSystemDefaultSt: Additional delay (in hours) stacked on top of the default delay for 'free' traits obtained at the start of the game. For example, starting with 9 Strength would usually grant Hoarder for free. If the Delayed Traits system is enabled, with a Default Delay of 240 and Additional Delay for Starting Traits of 480, you won't get Hoarder at the start. Instead, it goes into the table with a maximum delay of 720 hours. So, you'll have a 1 in 720 chance to get it in the first hour, and so on
Sandbox_ETW_DelayedTraitsSystem_tooltip:
This system allows you to gain/lose traits in a more random and less predictable way. When you qualify to add/remove a trait (list of affected traits can be found in the Google Sheets linked in the workshop description), it doesn't happen instantly. Instead, it's added to a table with a delay in hours (based on sandbox settings below). Every hour, the game rolls to determine if you gain/lose each trait in this table (each trait is rolled separately). You have a 1 in X chance to gain/lose the trait every hour. If the roll fails, X is reduced by 1. So the longer you go, the higher the chances that the trait you earned will manifest.
IMPORTANT: Traits that rely on skill levels will be unlocked as described above. However, traits that rely on internal counters added by this mod (e.g., Prone To Illness, Weak Stomach, Outdoorsman, Cat Eyes, etc.) will unlock the next time their internal counters increase. This is because coding a separate unlock mechanism for these specific traits would be complex and unnecessary. For example, if you qualify for Cat Eyes and it's added to the Delayed Traits Table, you won't get it immediately. Instead, you'll get it as soon as the Cat Eyes counter increases next time (e.g., the closest evening/night). Specific cases are noted on the Google Sheets

Sandbox_ETW_FearOfLocationsSystemCounter: Fear Of Locations Counter Decrease Multiplier

Controls how fast the counter decreases when you are unhappy (50%+) or stressed (50%+). Since you are more often normal than unhappy or stressed, it's recommended not to set this value too low. The formula for how much the counter decreases every minute is: 1 * (1 + unhappiness(0-1)) + (1 * stress(0-1)) * multiplier. At minimum, you lose 0 * multiplier; at maximum, you lose 4 * multiplier

Counter = amount of minutes you need to spend outside/inside to lose the respective trait, Agoraphobic or Claustrophobic. Being outside/inside when stressed or unhappy will decrease the counter. Reaching a negative counter value will GAIN you the appropriate trait. Reaching a positive counter value will get rid of the appropriate trait, being in between -counter and +counter will result in no change. The lower boundary is -2x of the counter, and the upper boundary is 2x of the set value here
Sandbox_ETW_FearOfLocationsSystemPassive: Fear of Locations Passive Counter Decay

If you have an issue managing both Agoraphobic and Claustrophobic traits simultaneously, you can set up passive decay using this option. This slowly decays the opposite counter based on whether you're outside/inside every minute, regardless of your mental state. For example, setting this to 0.1 and being inside will gradually reduce your fear of outside by adding 0.1 to the outside counter every minute. Setting it to 0 will have no effect on counters. Recommended values range from 0.1 to 0.5

Sandbox_ETW_SleepSystem_tooltip:
Enables obtaining/losing sleep-related traits, Wakeful and Sleepyhead.
---General description---
Mod tracks at which hours you like to sleep, and as long as you sleep within around those hours, you reinforce your healthy sleep schedule. If you sleep outside of it, you lose some schedule integrity. Not sleeping for more than 24 hours also starts to damage your sleep schedule. Mod tracks at which hours you like to sleep by checking the midpoint of your last sleep, so it's possible to shift your schedule to different hours.
---Technical description, don't read this if you don't want to meta-game much---
Every time you sleep, the mod tracks the midpoint of your sleep. For example, if you sleep between 23:00 and 05:30, the midpoint would be 03:45, and it would be your optimal sleeping hour. If you sleep within +-6 hours of this time, you gain progress to your SleepHealthiness bar, which ranges from -200 to 200. A check is made every 10 minutes, adding +0.167 to the bar if sleeping within the correct schedule, essentially giving +1 for every hour of correct sleep. Sleeping outside this schedule removes 0.167 from the bar every 10 minutes. After not sleeping for more than 24 hours, you start losing -0.167 every ten minutes. If your bar falls below -100, you get Sleepyhead. If it's above 100, you get Wakeful. Starting with either trait sets the starting value of the bar to its maximum or minimum point: +200 for Wakeful and -200 for Sleepyhead
Sandbox_ETW_SmokerCounter_tooltip:
Upon reaching this value on the counter, you'll get the Smoker trait. When dropping to the opposite of it, you'll lose it. Counter boundaries are -2x the counter to 2x the counter.

How it works: Every smoke you take increases your addiction counter. The longer breaks you take between smokes, the less the increase. Smoking while being panicked or stressed increases the counter much faster, based on the level of panic and stress. Not smoking slowly decays the counter. Being panicked or stressed slows the decay rate

How many hours (on average) should an alcoholic need to drink to sate their need?
Lower values mean Alcoholics need to drink frequently to avoid withdrawal.
Sandbox_MoreTraits_AlcoholicWithdrawal_t:
After how many hours without alcohol should an alcoholic start suffering withdrawal symptoms?
This value should be higher than Alcoholic Drink Frequency.
Sandbox_MoreTraits_AntiqueAnywhere_toolt: If true, Antique Collector trait will be able to find antique items anywhere, instead of only in crates and metal shelves.
Sandbox_MoreTraits_AntiqueChance_tooltip:
How rare Antique Collector items are.
This value is representative of a 1 in X roll.
Higher values mean drops happen less frequently.
Sandbox_MoreTraits_BatteringRamMartial_t: If this is enabled, knocking down zombies with Battering Ram trait will damage them if you have Martial Artist trait.
Sandbox_MoreTraits_BouncerCooldown_toolt:
How many ticks between Bouncer shoves.
60 ticks = 1 second
Reducing this value can dramatically overpower Bouncer.
Sandbox_MoreTraits_BouncerDistance_toolt:
The distance (in tile squares) from the player out to which Bouncer begins attempting to shove enemies away.
Lower values means Bouncer will take effect only at closer ranges to the player.
Sandbox_MoreTraits_BouncerEffectiveness_:
The percent chance that a player who has the Bouncer trait will trigger its effect.
This is rolled every tick, so 60 times/second for as long as the player has at least two or more zombies near them.
Increasing this value by even a small amount can dramatically overpower Bouncer.
Sandbox_MoreTraits_BurnedDistance_toolti:
Distance at which Burn Ward Patient gets activated by fire.
Keep in mind, the higher this value is, the more panic and anxiety you will get from being close.
Warning: Higher values might lead to FPS issues
Sandbox_MoreTraits_BurnedFireAversion_to: Should players with Burn Ward be forbidden from starting fires?
Sandbox_MoreTraits_BurnedPanic_tooltip:
Panic you will get from Burn Ward Patient.
Scaling works by repeatedly adding panic until max distance number is reached, starting from distance of closest fire.
What this means is, if fire is 1 tile away and max is 20, it will give 20x panic than this number. This happens every one minute in-game.
Sandbox_MoreTraits_BurnedStress_tooltip:
Stress you will get from Burn Ward Patient.
Scaling works by repeatedly adding stress until max distance number is reached, starting from distance of closest fire.
What this means is, if fire is 1 tile away and max is 20, it will give 20x stress than this number. This happens every one minute in-game.
Sandbox_MoreTraits_ButterfingersChance_t:
The chance in X how frequently a player with Butterfingers will drop an item.
The base chance is 5, so by default this value would be a 5/2000 chance calculated every in-game minute.
Lowering this value could result in players constantly dropping their items.
Sandbox_MoreTraits_EvasiveAnimation_tool: If enabled, Evasive has no "being hit" animation when it activates
Sandbox_MoreTraits_EvasiveBlocksPVP_tool: If true, Evasive can dodge attacks from other players.
Sandbox_MoreTraits_EvasiveChance_tooltip:
The percent chance that a player with the Evasive trait will dodge an attack.
Setting this to 100% will make players with Evasive immune to zombies.
Sandbox_MoreTraits_FastRunning_tooltip: Speed modifier applied when running with the 'Fast' trait.
Sandbox_MoreTraits_FastSprint_tooltip: Speed modifier applied when sprinting with the 'Fast' trait.
Sandbox_MoreTraits_FastWalking_tooltip: Speed modifier applied when walking with the 'Fast' trait.
Sandbox_MoreTraits_ForgivingDeprived_too:
Players who take the Deprived trait will still be allowed to keep a belt.
Belts are ordinarily difficult to obtain through normal play without killing other players or mods which add them to loot tables.
Sandbox_MoreTraits_GimpRunning_tooltip: Speed reduction applied when running with the 'Gimp' trait.
Sandbox_MoreTraits_GimpSprint_tooltip: Speed reduction applied when sprinting with the 'Gimp' trait.
Sandbox_MoreTraits_GimpWalking_tooltip: Speed reduction applied when walking with the 'Gimp' trait.
Sandbox_MoreTraits_GordaniteEffectivenes:
A percent value representing how effective the Gordanite trait makes players with Crowbars.
Setting this to 200% will make Gordanite twice as powerful.
Sandbox_MoreTraits_GraveRobberChance_too: The % chance that a player with the Grave Robber trait will find extra loot on a zombie corpse. Chance is rounded down to nearest 0.1%
Sandbox_MoreTraits_GraveRobberGuaranteed:
How much guaranteed extra loot will Grave Robber's find on zombie corpses?
By default, a random value is determined. Increasing this value guarantees extra loot whenever loot is rolled.
Sandbox_MoreTraits_GymGoerNoExerciseFati:
Should Gym Goer affect Exercise Fatigue?
If this option is un-checked, players with Gym Goer will suffer from exercise fatigue as normal.
Sandbox_MoreTraits_GymGoerPercent_toolti:
The percent amount extra Fitness/Strength XP a player receives from working out if they have the Gym Goer trait.
Setting this to 100% means no extra XP is gained.
Sandbox_MoreTraits_HardyEndurance_toolti: How much extra endurance should hardy give?
Sandbox_MoreTraits_HoarderCompatibility_:
If you run both More Traits and Expended Traits, they both have perks that update carry weight.
If this is checked on, Hoarder (from Expended Traits) and carry weight from this mod (Pack Mule/Mouse) will stack. Which means that whatever carry weight you get with Pack Mule/Pack Mouse will be multiplied by 1.25.
If this is checked off, and you have both perks (Hoarder and Pack Mule/Mouse) either Hoarder or Pack Mouse/Mule will run its code first meaing I honestly don't know if your max weight will be calculated by Hoarder code or Pack Mule code
Sandbox_MoreTraits_ImmunoChance_tooltip:
Chance for a player with Immunocompromised to get infected by a zombie attack.
Setting this value to 100 will make every zombie attack infectious.
Sandbox_MoreTraits_IncomprehensiveChance:
A percent value representing the chance that a player with the Incomprehensive trait will find fewer items in any container.
Setting this to 100% would make every container lose some items.
Sandbox_MoreTraits_IndefatigableOneUse_t:
If true, Indefatigable trait can be used only once per character.
If false, it will recharge based on the option below.
Note: If false, it will cure zombification only once.
Sandbox_MoreTraits_IndefatigableRecharge:
After how many in-game days should the indefatigable ability recharge?
Note: This option will only work if "Indefatigable One Use" option is disabled.
Sandbox_MoreTraits_IngenuitiveLimitAmoun:
What percent of unknown recipes should be learned by Ingenuitive?
This is randomly chosen out of all available recipes.
Sandbox_MoreTraits_IngenuitiveLimit_tool:
Limit the amount of free recipes granted by Ingenuitive?
By default, all recipes are taught for players who have Ingenuitive.
Turning this on allows you to limit how much they learn instead.
Sandbox_MoreTraits_InjuredBurns_tooltip:
Whether or not players who take the Injured trait can receive burns as a random injury.
Burns can be extremely debilitating especially if on the legs.
Sandbox_MoreTraits_LuckImpact_tooltip:
The percent degree to which Lucky and Unlucky traits affect traits from this mod.
0% means lucky and unlucky have no effect on traits.
Sandbox_MoreTraits_MartialScaling_toolti:
A percent value representing how strong the scaling in damage for Martial Artist is.
Setting this to 50% would halve all damage.
Sandbox_MoreTraits_MartialWeapons_toolti:
By default, Martial Artist will only deal damage if the player is unarmed.
If enabled, players' shoves will still deal damage even if they are wielding a weapon.
If this option is disabled, you can still benefit from Martial Artist with an item in your Secondary slot (Flashlight, Bag, etc). It only checks if the Primary slot is equipped.
Sandbox_MoreTraits_NonlethalAlcoholic_to: Players who take the alcoholic trait can never die from alcohol poisoning, but will still suffer periodic withdrawal.
Sandbox_MoreTraits_PreparedCarGasToggle_: Players who choose the Prepared: Cars trait start with a gas can.
Sandbox_MoreTraits_PreparedMedicalBandag: The amount of bandages given by the Prepare Medical trait.
Sandbox_MoreTraits_ProwessGunsAmmoRestor: If true, Prowess: Guns will have a chance to not consume ammo. Not exactly lore-wise friendly, but fun.
Sandbox_MoreTraits_QuickSuperImmune_tool:
If true, Super Immune is 6 times faster than normal.
Preferably used in multiplayer games where you can't fast forward time.
Sandbox_MoreTraits_QuickWorkerScaler_too:
Determines how much faster players with the Quick Worker trait perform actions.
Set this to 50 for a 50% reduction in time (twice as fast).
Set to 10 for a 10% reduction.
Default: 50.
Sandbox_MoreTraits_ScroungerChance_toolt:
A percent value representing the chance that a player with the Scrounger trait will find extra loot in any container.
100% would make every container have extra loot.
Sandbox_MoreTraits_ScroungerItemChance_t:
A percent value representing how likely any item in a container is to be duplicated by Scrounger.
For example: Scrounger Chance is the percent chance that any given container will potentially have extra loot in it.
Once a container passes this initial check, another series of checks is run - for every single item inside that container, a die is rolled to determine if that item is duplicated.
If for instance, this value is set to 100%, then whenever Scrounger is triggered, the entire contents of that container will be duplicated.
If Scrounger Chance, and Scrounger Item Chance are both 100%, then anyone with the Scrounger trait will find double the loot in every container.
Sandbox_MoreTraits_ScroungerLootModifier:
A percent value representing how much extra loot players with the Scrounger trait will find when Scrounger triggers.
100% would double the amount of extra loot players find.
This value always rounds up, so even a single item can potentially be doubled.
This effect is most noticed in stacks of items such as cigarettes or ammunition.
Sandbox_MoreTraits_SecondWindCooldown_to: After how much days should Second Wind recharge?
Sandbox_MoreTraits_SlowWorkerScaler_tool:
Determines how much slower players with the Slow Worker trait perform actions.
Set this to 50 for a 50% increase in time (1.5x longer).
Set to 100 for a 100% increase (twice as long).
Default: 50.
Sandbox_MoreTraits_SpecializationXPPerce:
The percent value that XP is reduced by for those who take a Specialization trait.
Set to 0% to disable XP reduction.
Sandbox_MoreTraits_SuperImmuneFirstInfec: Super Immune Less Time After Curing

If true, any infections after the first one was cured will only last half as long.
Note: If caught multiple infections, it can still reach maximum days.
Sandbox_MoreTraits_SuperImmuneMaximum_to: Maximum days that a Super Immune character takes to heal the sickness.
Sandbox_MoreTraits_SuperImmuneMinimum_to: Minimum days that a Super Immune character takes to heal the sickness.
Sandbox_MoreTraits_SuperImmuneWeakness_t: If this setting is on, Super Immune will not be able to defend your body if it caught several infections.
Sandbox_MoreTraits_UnwaveringDamageBoost:
Determines the damage boost received when certain conditions are met. Damage scales based on the following:

Tier 1 (Default 2.0x): Endurance < 25%, Fatigue > 80%, or Pain > 75.
Tier 2 (Default 1.5x): Endurance < 50%, Fatigue > 70%, or Pain > 50.
Tier 3 (Default 1.25x): Endurance < 75%, Fatigue > 60%, or Pain > 20.

If multiple conditions are met, only the highest tier applies.
Sandbox_MoreTraits_VagabondChance_toolti:
A percent value representing the chance that a player with the Vagabond trait will find extra food in a garbage bin.
Setting this to 100% would make every trash bin contain extra food.
Sandbox_MoreTraits_VagabondGuaranteedExt:
How many extra food items is a player with the Vagabond trait guaranteed to find in a garbage bin?
By default, a random value between 0 and 2 is selected. This value adds to that range.
(eg; roll a random number between 0 and 2 and then add x, where x is this value.)
Sandbox_MoreTraits_WeightDefault_tooltip:
Defines the base inventory carry capacity for players with neither Pack Mule nor Pack Mouse traits.
This is the base value before applying bonuses from Strength.
Sandbox_MoreTraits_WeightGlobalMod_toolt:
Gives bonus inventory carry capacity to all players.
You can set a negative value to reduce all player inventory capacity globally as well.
Sandbox_MoreTraits_WeightPackMouse_toolt:
Defines the base inventory carry capacity for those who take the Pack Mouse trait.
This is the base value before applying bonuses from Strength.
Sandbox_MoreTraits_WeightPackMule_toolti:
Defines the base inventory carry capacity for those who take the Pack Mule trait.
This is the base value before applying bonuses from Strength.

Sandbox_SIMBA_TSY_AlertNearbyZombies_too: If enabled, the scream uses the game’s sound system to alert zombies within a set radius. If disabled, the scream is only aesthetic — it scares the player but doesn’t affect horde AI. Great for immersive sound without changing difficulty.
Sandbox_SIMBA_TSY_AlertRadius_tooltip: The size of the area, in tiles, where zombies will hear the scream and walk toward the source. Example: 20 affects just the nearby street; 80–100 can wake up half the neighborhood. Very high values make every scream a mini-horde event.
Sandbox_SIMBA_TSY_ChaseCooldownHours_too: Minimum in-game hours between screams from the SAME sprinter during a chase. 0.01 ≈ about 36 in-game seconds. Higher values make chases quieter; lower ones make them louder and more frantic.
Sandbox_SIMBA_TSY_ChaseScreechChance_too: Extra chance for a scream while the sprinter is already chasing you. Use low values (5–20) for occasional screams and higher ones for chaotic, noisy pursuits. Combine this with the cooldown setting to avoid endless screaming.
Sandbox_SIMBA_TSY_ClusterCooldownHours_t: Duration of the time window controlling screams per group (cluster), in in-game hours. Within this window, the total number of screams is limited by 'Max screams per group'. Example: 0.01 ≈ about 36 in-game seconds for that area.
Sandbox_SIMBA_TSY_ClusterRadius_tooltip: Defines the size of the area considered a single screaming scene. If multiple sprinters are within this radius, they count as one group (cluster). The scream limit per group applies to all of them, preventing endless chaos in crowded areas.
Sandbox_SIMBA_TSY_EnableNearScream_toolt: When enabled, the sprinter emits an extra scream when entering close range (face scream), in addition to the discovery and chase screams. Disabling it reduces audio chaos in melee combat and keeps the mod cleaner in dense hordes.
Sandbox_SIMBA_TSY_FarRange_tooltip: Maximum distance in tiles at which the discovery scream can be heard in 3D. Higher values make the sound travel farther, warning you of sprinters before you see them. Useful in open maps where echoes enhance immersion.
Sandbox_SIMBA_TSY_FarVolume_tooltip: Controls the volume of the discovery scream when the sprinter is still far away. Example: 0.1 gives a faint echo; values near 1.0 make the scream stand out even from a distance.
Sandbox_SIMBA_TSY_GlobalVolume_tooltip: General volume multiplier applied to all screams in the mod. 1.0 = default; 0.5 = half volume; 2.0 = double volume (careful with your ears). Use this to balance the mod’s audio with the rest of the game.
Sandbox_SIMBA_TSY_MaxClusterScreams_tool: Maximum number of screams allowed in each group during the set window. Example: with value 2, even if 10 sprinters spot you at once, only 2 screams will be heard in that scene.
Sandbox_SIMBA_TSY_MaxProcessDistance_too: Maximum distance in tiles from the player within which theySEE YOU still processes sprinters for screams. Zombies beyond this radius are ignored to save performance. Values between 180 and 250 maintain immersion while staying efficient.
Sandbox_SIMBA_TSY_NearAsWorldSound_toolt: If enabled, the close scream uses PlayWorldSound just like the distant one — full 3D sound with spatial direction and echo, perfect for headsets. If disabled, it uses the zombie’s local audio, sharper and more direct.
Sandbox_SIMBA_TSY_NearVolume_tooltip: Volume multiplier when the sprinter screams right in your face. For a stronger jump scare, use 1.0 or higher; high values make screams much more aggressive.
Sandbox_SIMBA_TSY_ScreechChance_tooltip: Probability that a sprinter will let out a discovery scream the first time they see you. 0 = never screams; 100 = almost every sprinter screams as soon as they spot you. Example: 60 means roughly 6 out of 10 encounters start with a scream.
Sandbox_SIMBA_TSY_TickRate_tooltip: How many ticks the game waits before reprocessing each sprinter. Lower values = faster reaction but more CPU load; higher = lighter but slower. 6 is a recommended balance for large hordes and helicopter events.
Sandbox_SIMBA_TSY_TimeMode_tooltip: Defines at what time of day sprinters can use their special scream. 0 = day and night; 1 = day only; 2 = night only. Example: use '2' for calmer days and much more tense nights.

Set to 0 to disable.

Sandbox_StarvingZombies_AllowWornAttache:
When enabled (checked), worn/attached items will be visible on the skeleton.
This can look weird.
Sandbox_StarvingZombies_BaseEatTime_tool:
This value is used to calculate how long a body takes to be eaten.
It is in real world seconds.
The actual time taken to consume a corpse is adjusted by:
- Size of the body relative(ish) to a human.
- Number of zombies eating the body.
- Current game speed.
Sandbox_StarvingZombies_BodyAgeMultiplie:
Bodies will have their age (world hours) multiplied by this value, which is then added to Start Distance.
Any body within this distance will be selected as a possible target.
Sandbox_StarvingZombies_BodyDispersionAn:
This angle is used to determine whether a zombie falls inside an area that is projected from bodies in the direction of the wind, up to a distance of [Start Distance + Age Multiplier + Wind Speed Multiplier].
Set to 0 to disable, causing wind direction to have no effect.
Sandbox_StarvingZombies_BodyIndoorCheck_: When enabled (checked), only zombies in the same building as a body are capable of smelling it.
Sandbox_StarvingZombies_BodyMaxDistance_: Any body further than this distance from a zombie will never be selected as a target.
Sandbox_StarvingZombies_BodySizeImpactsS: When enabled (checked), the size of the body has an effect on the distance.
Sandbox_StarvingZombies_BodyStartDistanc: Any body within this distance from a zombie will always be selected as a possible target.
Sandbox_StarvingZombies_BodyTemperatureP:
This value is divided by the current temperature, to get a multiplier that adjusts the result of [Start Distance + Age Multiplier].
This value is in celsius.
Set to 0 to disable temperature having an effect.
At default, temperatures above 26C increase the distance bodies can be smelled by zombies, while temperatures below 26C decrease the distance.
Sandbox_StarvingZombies_BodyWindSpeedMul: The current wind speed (km/h) is multiplied by this value, which is then added to the result of [Start Distance + Age Multiplier].
Sandbox_StarvingZombies_CanSmellPlayer_t: When enabled (checked), zombies will be able to smell players.
Sandbox_StarvingZombies_ItemDestroyChanc: When a body is consumed, every individual item has this chance of being destroyed.
Sandbox_StarvingZombies_PlayerBloodMulti: The amount of blood on a player, and on items in their inventory, will be multiplied by this value, which is then added to Start Distance.
Sandbox_StarvingZombies_PlayerDispersion:
This angle is used to determine whether a zombie falls inside an area that is projected from players in the direction of the wind, up to a distance of [Start Distance + Blood Multiplier + Wind Speed Multiplier].
Set to 0 disable, causing wind direction to have no effect.
Sandbox_StarvingZombies_PlayerMaxDistanc: Any player further than this distance from a zombie will never be selected as a target.
Sandbox_StarvingZombies_PlayerStartDista: Any player within this distance from a zombie will always be targeted.
Sandbox_StarvingZombies_PlayerWindSpeedM: The current wind speed (km/h) is multiplied by this value, which is then added to the result of [Start Distance + Blood Multiplier].

Sandbox_VorpallySauced_EnableCritAffixes: Allow affixes that boost critical chance and critical damage (Precise, Deadly...). Default: On.
Sandbox_VorpallySauced_EnableDamageAffix: Allow affixes that increase weapon damage (Sharp, Brutal, of Harm...). Default: On.
Sandbox_VorpallySauced_EnableDebugLogs_t: Troubleshooting only. With PZ debug mode active, print detailed VorpallySauced messages to the console. Default: Off.
Sandbox_VorpallySauced_EnableDurabilityA: Allow affixes that raise max condition and slow down wear. Default: On.
Sandbox_VorpallySauced_EnableFireAffixes: Allow affixes that set zombies on fire on hit (Smoldering, Scorching, Immolating). Fire can spread to nearby things, use PZ's own Fire Spread setting to control that. Default: On.
Sandbox_VorpallySauced_EnableFireRateAff: Allow firearm affixes that increase fire rate. Turn off if another mod controls fire rate. Default: On.
Sandbox_VorpallySauced_EnableFirearmAffi: Let guns earn upgrades from kills, the same way melee weapons do. Off = only melee weapons progress and guns stay vanilla. Already-upgraded guns keep their stats. Default: On.
Sandbox_VorpallySauced_EnableLegendaryAf: Allow the rare multi-stat Legendary affixes to roll. To keep them but make them rarer, lower Legendary Affix Frequency instead. Default: On.
Sandbox_VorpallySauced_EnableMod_tooltip: Turn the whole weapon mastery system on or off. Off = weapons behave exactly like vanilla. Default: On.
Sandbox_VorpallySauced_EnableRangeAffixe: Allow affixes that extend melee reach and firearm range. Default: On.
Sandbox_VorpallySauced_EnableReloadAffix: Allow firearm affixes that make reloading faster. Default: On.
Sandbox_VorpallySauced_EnableStaggerAffi: Allow affixes that stagger zombies on hit (Forceful, Crushing, of Impact...). Turn off if another combat mod already handles stagger. Default: On.
Sandbox_VorpallySauced_EnableTraitEffect: On = weapon personality traits (Nocturnal, Ravenous, Patient...) give real bonuses such as stamina, calm, crit, and durability, growing stronger as the weapon deepens. Off = traits are flavor only. Default: On.
Sandbox_VorpallySauced_EnableUtilityAffi: Allow assorted affixes: attack speed, stamina, multi-hit, knockback, door damage, jam chance, magazine size, and noise. Default: On.
Sandbox_VorpallySauced_HideFlavorText_to: Hide the atmospheric lore lines on weapon tooltips (e.g. 'Something stirs within'). Stats and upgrade info still show. Default: Off.
Sandbox_VorpallySauced_LegendaryChance_t: How often legendary (multi-stat) affixes roll, compared to the other tiers. Higher = more common. Set to 0 to switch legendaries off entirely. Default: 5.
Sandbox_VorpallySauced_NotifyOnUpgrade_t: Show a message when a weapon earns a new upgrade. Default: On.
Sandbox_VorpallySauced_PrefixKillsMax_to: Most kills before a weapon earns its first upgrade, a Mark. Default: 29.
Sandbox_VorpallySauced_PrefixKillsMin_to: Fewest kills before a weapon can earn its first upgrade, a Mark. The real number is rolled between min and max; fragile weapons need fewer. Default: 10.
Sandbox_VorpallySauced_ShardOnFullBondin: Give a Vorpal Shard when a weapon becomes fully Blooded. Shards re-roll the upgrades on any weapon. Default: On.
Sandbox_VorpallySauced_ShowKillProgress_: On = weapon tooltips show the exact kills left until the next upgrade. Off = tooltips show atmospheric hints instead. Default: Off.
Sandbox_VorpallySauced_StirredDurability: Minimum effective durability once a weapon earns its first upgrade, so fragile weapons (screwdrivers, kitchen knives) survive long enough to keep progressing. Higher = more protection. Sturdy weapons are unaffected. Default: 5.
Sandbox_VorpallySauced_SuffixKillsMax_to: Most kills before a weapon earns its second upgrade, a Bond. Default: 70.
Sandbox_VorpallySauced_SuffixKillsMin_to: Fewest kills before a weapon earns its second upgrade, a Bond. Default: 30.
Sandbox_VorpallySauced_Tier1Weight_toolt: How often common (Tier 1) affixes roll, compared to the other tiers. Higher = more common. The four frequency numbers are weighed against each other, they are not percentages. Default: 40.
Sandbox_VorpallySauced_Tier2Weight_toolt: How often uncommon (Tier 2) affixes roll, compared to the other tiers. Higher = more common. Default: 30.
Sandbox_VorpallySauced_Tier3Weight_toolt: How often rare (Tier 3) affixes roll, compared to the other tiers. Higher = more common. Default: 20.
Sandbox_VorpallySauced_TraitEffectStreng: How strong trait bonuses are: 1 = Subtle (50%), 2 = Moderate (75%), 3 = Full (100%). Only used when Trait Gameplay Effects is on. Default: 3 (Full).
Sandbox_VorpallySauced_TraitNotification: Floating messages for weapon personality. 0 = off, 1 = trait changes only, 2 = traits and mood shifts. Traits always appear on tooltips regardless. Default: 2 (traits and moods).
Sandbox_WZHordeZombie_AllowCrawlers_tool: When enabled (checked), crawlers are allowed to be in hordes.
Sandbox_WZHordeZombie_AllowFastShamblers: When enabled (checked), fast shamblers are allowed to be in hordes.
Sandbox_WZHordeZombie_AllowShamblers_too: When enabled (checked), shamblers are allowed to be in hordes.
Sandbox_WZHordeZombie_AllowSprinters_too: When enabled (checked), sprinters are allowed to be in hordes.
Sandbox_WZHordeZombie_CreateJoinMergeDis: The maximum distance at which zombies will create/join/merge hordes.
Sandbox_WZHordeZombie_GroupBySpeed_toolt:
Zombies can only join hordes that match their speed.
Only reads the speedType variable, so mods that alter the animation speeds and do not correctly set the zombie's speedType to match will conflict.
Sandbox_WZHordeZombie_HordeLimit_tooltip:
The maximum number of hordes that are allowed to exist.
Set to 0 to disable.
Sandbox_WZHordeZombie_HordeSize_tooltip:
The largest size a horde can grow to.
When merging is enabled, this is ignored.
Set this value to 0 to allow hordes to be of any size.
Sandbox_WZHordeZombie_HordesMerge_toolti: When enabled (checked), hordes can join together to become a single, bigger horde.
Sandbox_WZHordeZombie_Hordes_tooltip: When enabled (checked), Wandering Zombie's horde features will turn on.
Sandbox_WZHordeZombie_LeaveDistance_tool:
Zombies that are at least (Leave Distance + Create/Join/Merge Distance) away from the horde will leave the horde.
Set to 0 to disable.
Sandbox_WZHordeZombie_MergeCooldown_tool:
The amount of time that must pass before a horde is allowed to merge.
This value is multiplied by the number of followers in a horde.
Sandbox_WZHordeZombie_MoveCooldownHordeS: This value is multiplied by the size of the horde before being added to the result of the above settings.
Sandbox_WZHordeZombie_WorldAgeHours_tool: Hordes will not start to form until the world is at least this old.
Sandbox_WZPerformance_SandboxVarsUpdateF:
Controls the frequency (milliseconds) of Wandering Zombies updating it's Sandbox Options.
Time periods and destruction are also calculated during this update.
You should not need to change this value.
Sandbox_WZPerformance_ZombieProcessLimit:
The number of zombies that Wandering Zombies performs an update on each frame.
This does not limit the amount of zombies Wandering Zombies has control over, as the update continues where it stopped on the last frame.
You should not need to change this value.
Sandbox_WZShared_HoursSurvived_tooltip: The minimum amount of hours a player needs to of survived for before zombies will be able to use the player as a target of homing.
Sandbox_WZShared_MoveCooldownInitial_too:
When enabled (checked), the move cooldown is applied to a zombie the moment Wandering Zombies becomes aware of it.
This will prevent zombies immediately moving the moment they load in. (From starting/loading a game, moving around the world, being teleported (Admin / RV Interiors)).
Sandbox_WZShared_TryStopVirtual_tooltip:
When enabled (checked), zombie movement will be monitored to try and prevent them from walking out of the loaded area.
This does not apply to sprinters due to the possibility of sprinters keeping up with the player, resulting in an increase over time of the sprinters in the area.
Sandbox_WZShared_WanderForwards_tooltip:
When enabled (checked), the random location chosen for wandering will be locked to a position +/- 45 degrees in the direction the zombie is facing.
Highly recommend Try Stop Virtual when using this option, to prevent all the zombies leaving the area.
Since this still chooses a random location and passes it to the game's finding, zombies won't necessarily move forwards due to fences, buildings, etc.
Sandbox_WanderingZombies_FleeChanceDropd:
The chance that zombies will move away from the player.

Static: The value chosen below is the value that is always used.
Random: A random value from Min to Max (inclusive) is chosen at random.
The value is randomised when the current period of activity has elasped (Start Hour to [Start Hour + Total Hours]).
If Min is greater than Max, Max will be used as the minimum.
Sandbox_WanderingZombies_FleeCooldownHou:
The amount of time that must elapse before the next period of activity.

Static: The value chosen below is the value that is always used.
Random: A random value from Min to Max (inclusive) is chosen at random.
The value is randomised when the current period of activity has elapsed (Start Hour to [Start Hour + Total Hours]).
If Min is greater than Max, Max will be used as the minimum.
Sandbox_WanderingZombies_FleeRadiusDropd:
Zombies that are at least this close to the player are allowed to use the flee settings.

Static: The value chosen below is the value that is always used.
Random: A random value from Min to Max (inclusive) is chosen at random.
The value is randomised when the current period of activity has elapsed (Start hour to [Start Hour + Total Hours]).
If Min is greater than Max, Max will be used as the minimum.
Sandbox_WanderingZombies_FleeRadiusInter:
When enabled, zombies that leave the Flee Radius are forcefully told to stop moving.

Random: When the period of activity elapses, it is randomised to either on or off.
Sandbox_WanderingZombies_FleeStartHourDr:
The hour at which fleeing can start.

Static: The value chosen below is the value that is always used.
Random: A random value from Min to Max (inclusive) is chosen at random.
The value is randomised when the current period of activity has elapsed (Start Hour to [Start Hour + Total Hours]).
Min is allowed to be greater than Max, so ranges over midnight can be used.
Sandbox_WanderingZombies_FleeTotalHoursD:
This is added to Start Hour to determine the period of activity.

Static: The value chosen below is the value that is always used.
Random: A random value from Min to Max (inclusive) is chosen at random.
The value is randomised when the current period of activity has elapsed (Start Hour to [Start Hour + Total Hours]).
If Min is greater than Max, Max will be used as the minimum.
Sandbox_WanderingZombies_HomingChanceDro:
The chance that zombies will move towards the player.

Static: The value chosen below is the value that is always used.
Random: A random value from Min to Max (inclusive) is chosen at random.
The value is randomised when the current period of activity has elasped (Start Hour to [Start Hour + Total Hours]).
If Min is greater than Max, Max will be used as the minimum.
Sandbox_WanderingZombies_HomingCooldownH:
The amount of time that must elapse before the next period of activity.

Static: The value chosen below is the value that is always used.
Random: A random value from Min to Max (inclusive) is chosen at random.
The value is randomised when the current period of activity has elapsed (Start Hour to [Start Hour + Total Hours]).
If Min is greater than Max, Max will be used as the minimum.
Sandbox_WanderingZombies_HomingRadiusDro:
Zombies that are more than this distance away from the player are allowed to use the homing settings.

Static: The value chosen below is the value that is always used.
Random: A random value from Min to Max (inclusive) is chosen at random.
The value is randomised when the current period of activity has elapsed (Start hour to [Start Hour + Total Hours]).
If Min is greater than Max, Max will be used as the minimum.
Sandbox_WanderingZombies_HomingRadiusInt:
When enabled, homing zombies that enter the Homing Radius are forcefully told to stop moving.

Random: When the period of activity elapses, it is randomised to either on or off.
Sandbox_WanderingZombies_HomingStartHour:
The hour at which homing can start.

Static: The value chosen below is the value that is always used.
Random: A random value from Min to Max (inclusive) is chosen at random.
The value is randomised when the current period of activity has elapsed (Start Hour to [Start Hour + Total Hours]).
Min is allowed to be greater than Max, so ranges over midnight can be used.
Sandbox_WanderingZombies_HomingTotalHour:
This is added to Start Hour to determine the period of activity.

Static: The value chosen below is the value that is always used.
Random: A random value from Min to Max (inclusive) is chosen at random.
The value is randomised when the current period of activity has elapsed (Start Hour to [Start Hour + Total Hours]).
If Min is greater than Max, Max will be used as the minimum.
Sandbox_WanderingZombies_MaxTravel_toolt:
A random value from 0 to Max Travel Distance (inclusive) is used to select a location, which is passed to the game's pathfinding.
Due to using the game's pathfinding, the resulting path may be longer than this value when needing to path around buildings, fences, etc.
This applies to Wander, Homing and Flee.
Sandbox_WanderingZombies_MoveCooldownRan:
A random value from 0 to Cooldown: Random (inclusive) is added to the above.
This value is randomised again during the resets mentioned in Cooldown's tooltip.
Sandbox_WanderingZombies_MoveCooldown_to:
The static amount of time (milliseconds) that must pass before a zombie is allowed to move.
Only applies to lone zombies and leaders of a horde, followers in a horde move when the leader does.
The cooldown is reset whenever a zombie is considered busy:
- While a zombie is moving (vanilla wandering is ignored)
- While a zombie has a target (character, dead body or thumpable)
Sandbox_WanderingZombies_WanderChanceDro:
The chance that zombies will move randomly.

Static: The value chosen below is the value that is always used.
Random: A random value from Min to Max (inclusive) is chosen at random.
The value is randomised when the current period of activity has elapsed (Start Hour to [Start Hour + Total Hours].
If Min is greater than Max, Max will be used as the minimum.
Sandbox_WanderingZombies_WanderCooldownH:
The amount of time that must elapse before the next period of activity.
If this value is not a multiple of 24, Start Hour will be modified by the remaining hours.
For example, if Start Hour is set to 0 (midnight), and Cooldown Hours is set to 26, the next Start Hour would be 2am.

Static: The value chosen is the value that is always used.
Random: A random value from Min to Max (inclusive) is chosen at random.
The value is randomised when the current period of activity has elasped (Start Hour to [Start Hour + Total Hours]).
If Min is greater than Max, Max is used as the minimum.
Sandbox_WanderingZombies_WanderDestructi:
Always: Zombies will always attack objects in their path.
Indoors: Only zombies considered indoors will attack objects in their path.
Never: Zombies will not attack objects in their path.
Random: When the period of activity elapses, one of the above three options will be chosen at random.

The vanilla destruction settings do NOT have an effect on this setting.
Sandbox_WanderingZombies_WanderStartHour:
The hour at which random wandering can start.

Static: The value chosen below is the value that is always used.
Random: A random value from Min to Max (inclusive) is chosen at random.
The value is randomised when the current period of activity has elapsed (Start Hour to [Start Hour + Total Hours].
Min is allowed to be greater than Max, so ranges over midnight can be used.

Random: A random value from Min to Max (inclusive) is chosen at random.
The value is randomised when the current period of activity has elapsed (Start Hour to [Start Hour + Total Hours]).
If Min is greater than Max, Max will be used as the minimum.
Sandbox_WayMoreCars: More Car Features + Spawn Zones Expansion
Sandbox_WhereAmI_HideUIOffRegion_tooltip: If enabled, the region indicator will not appear at all when a Player is outside any known regions.
Sandbox_XPGain_tooltip: How MUCH xp you gain. 1 = .25xp, default of 2 = .5xp
Sandbox_XPonBackstab_AdvancedXPBonus_too: This XP value will apply at Sneak Level 5-6
Sandbox_XPonBackstab_BeginnerXPBonus_too: This XP value will apply at Sneak Level 1-2
Sandbox_XPonBackstab_ExpertXPBonus_toolt: This XP value will apply at Sneak Level 7-8
Sandbox_XPonBackstab_IntermediateXPBonus: This XP value will apply at Sneak Level 3-4
Sandbox_XPonBackstab_MasterXPBonus_toolt: This XP value will apply at Sneak Level 9-10
Sandbox_ZombieOnly_tooltip: Enable this if you want the Proximity Inventory to work only on zombies
Sandbox_ZuperCarts_SpawnCarts_tooltip: Enable or disable the spawning of shopping carts in the world.
Sandbox_ZuperCarts_SpawnTrolleys_tooltip: Enable or disable the spawning of trolleys in the world.
Sandbox_pointblankhf_PointBlankBonusDama: Point Blank Bonus Damage Multiplier (ONLY USED WHEN InstantKill is DISABLED)

This multiplier is applied to your bonus damage from above. This does NOT affect BASE damage value, ONLY bonus damage. See equation below: 

 TotalDamage = BaseDamage + PointBlankDamage
 PointBlankDamage = (Max(BaseDamage,PointBlankMinimumBonusDamage) * PointBlankBonusDamageMultiplier )
Sandbox_pointblankhf_PointBlankDisableMu: Point Blank - Disable Multiplayer Command Updates

For debugging purposes. You shouldn't change this. But if you're having server/client/networking issues, this will disable synchronous server/client updates. Without this, when you pointblank kill a zed, there can be a delay for them appear dead for other players.
Sandbox_pointblankhf_PointBlankEffective: Zombies shot upon below this range will be affected by point blank damage
Sandbox_pointblankhf_PointBlankInstantKi:
If true, when shooting a zombie within point blank range, they die instantly. 
 If this is FALSE, the values below help determine bonus damage
Sandbox_pointblankhf_PointBlankMinTicksB: Point Blank - Min Ticks Between Point Blank Kills
"You can set this higher to enforce a "cooldown" between point blank kills. By default, this ensures point blank cant trigger multiple times in a single tick"
Sandbox_pointblankhf_PointBlankMinimumBo: Point Blank Minimum Bonus Damage (ONLY USED WHEN InstantKill is DISABLED)

This is the minimum amount bonus damage that is applied. 
IE, if your damage is .0001, it would increase the bonus damage to .2 
 See equation below: 

 TotalDamage = BaseDamage + PointBlankDamage
 PointBlankDamage = (Max(BaseDamage,PointBlankMinimumBonusDamage) * PointBlankBonusDamageMultiplier )
Sandbox_pointblankhf_PointBlankUseExperi: If true, hit detection is WAY more lenient. It won't matter if you are moving, or how long you have been aiming, they will just die, if within range (or point blank damage will take effect).
Sandbox_pointblankhf_PointBlankXpMultipl: Any xp gains from killing a zombie will be multiplied by this value
Sandbox_sts_MaxDaysInMonthFall: How many days per month during fall(Sep - Nov)?

Sandbox_sts_MaxDaysInMonthSpring: How many days per month during spring(Mar - May)?

Sandbox_sts_MaxDaysInMonthWinter: How many days per month during winter(Dec - Feb)?
